publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:30:14 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

ead.archdesc:
======= RMM04285International War Crimes Tribunal records4285Compiled by T.A. GobertDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
July 21, 20032B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
vnwar seasiThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:48:33 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
International War Crimes Tribunal records
no primary creator
International War Crimes Tribunal (1st ) (Location of meeting: Stockholm, Sweden and Roskilde, Denmark). Date of meeting or treaty signing: 1967 :.)
4285
2.4 cubic feet. 2.4 cubic feet.
1967, 1970. Statements, correspondence, reports, testimonies, photographs. Tribunal records include mimeographs, edited versions, and final drafts of testimonies, reports, opening and closing statements, and summaries of the Stockholm and Copenhagen sessions (1967); also, press releases, copies of correspondence, photographs, speeches, agendas, and statutes. In addition, there are materials pertaining to the National Liberation Front; reports of the Commission for the Denunciation of U.S. War Crimes in South Vietnam (1970); and reports, printed matter, and other materials concerning the International Commission of Enquiry into U.S. Crimes in Indochina. Collection material in English 2084387
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
T.A. Gobert
Date completed:
July 21, 1987
EAD encoding:
Ann Hubert, July 2003
Date modified:
Jude Corina, January 2014
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
The International War Crimes Tribunal was sponsored by the Bertrand Russell Peace Foundation to provide a forum for testimony and evidence concerning the damaging effects of U.S. policy in Indochina.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Tribunal records include mimeographs, edited versions, and final drafts of testimonies, reports, opening and closing statements, and summaries of the Stockholm and Copenhagen sessions (1967); also, press releases, copies of correspondence, photographs, speeches, agendas, and statutes. In addition, there are materials pertaining to the National Liberation Front; reports of the Commission for the Denunciation of U.S. War Crimes in South Vietnam (1970); and reports, printed matter, and other materials concerning the International Commission of Enquiry into U.S. Crimes in Indochina.
Cite As:
International War Crimes Tribunal records, #4285. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Testimonies. Photographs. Thailand. Laos. Cambodia. War -- Protection of civilians. War (International law) War crimes. Vietnamese Conflict, 1961-1975 -- Chemical warfare. Vietnamese Conflict, 1961-1975 -- Atrocities. Prisoners of war. Biological warfare. Against the crime of silence: proceedings of the Russell International War Crimes Tribunal. Behar, Abraham. Burchett, Wilfred G., 1911-1983. Caldwell, Malcolm. Chaliand, Gérard, 1934- Chesneaux, Jean. Faulkner, Stanley. Fourniau, Charles. Gaulle, Charles de, 1890-1970. Halimi, Gisèle. Kolko, Gabriel. Kaisō̧n Phomvihān. Norodom Sihanouk, Prince, 1922-2012. Pic, Roger. Riffaud, Madeleine. Russell, Bertrand, 1872-1970. Sartre, Jean Paul, 1905- Thanh Savanvilay. Thiounn Prasith. Bertrand Russell Peace Foundation Commission for the Denunciation of U.S. War Crimes in South Vietnam International Commission of Enquiry into U.S. Crimes in Indochina Mạ̮t trận dân tộc giải phóng miè̂n nam Việt Nam Vietnam (Democratic Republic). Ủy ban điè̂u tra tội ác chi é̂n tranh của đè̂ quó̂c Mỹ ở Việt-Nam Vietnam (Provisional Revolutionary Government, 1969-1975)
